edges.description: Reduced ATGL activity causes triglyceride deposition in heart and vessel cells.
edges.source: Reduced ATGL Activity Without PNPLA2 Mutation

nodes.description: Triglyceride accumulates in cardiomyocytes and coronary vascular cells, impairing cardiac energy metabolism and driving cardiomyopathy and coronary artery disease, as in primary TGCV.

evidence.reference_title: Triglyceride deposit cardiomyovasculopathy: a rare cardiovascular disorder.
evidence.snippet:
adult-onset severe heart disease, including heart failure and coronary artery disease, associated with a
marked reduction in ATGL activity and myocardial washout rate of LCFA tracer

nodes.description: Patients with idiopathic TGCV have a marked reduction in adipose triglyceride lipase (ATGL) activity despite the absence of PNPLA2 mutations; the etiology of the acquired enzyme deficiency is not yet known.
nodes.id: Reduced ATGL Activity Without PNPLA2 Mutation

evidence.reference_title: Triglyceride deposit cardiomyovasculopathy: a rare cardiovascular disorder.
evidence.snippet:
Patients with I-TGCV, of which etiologies and causes are not known yet, suffered from adult-onset severe heart disease, including heart failure and coronary artery disease, associated with a
marked reduction in ATGL activity
